registry.description: Sensuality is the dimension of human experience that is directly tied to bodily sensation and pleasure — the realm of taste, touch, smell, beauty, and physical delight. The Greek vocabulary here (aselgeia, debauchery) tends to describe the disordered form of this; the wider biblical picture includes a positive account of created pleasure (the Song of Solomon, the goodness of creation). Sensuality is not inherently sinful but becomes disordered when it displaces other goods or pursues pleasure as an end in itself. [XREF: covered by flesh (#185).]

terms.lsj_entry:
ἀσέλγ-εια, ἡ, 
licentiousness, wanton violence, [Refs 5th c.BC+]; insolence, opposed to κολακεία, [Refs 4th c.BC+]
__II licentiousness, περὶ τὰς σωματικὰς ἐπιθυμίας[Refs 2nd c.BC+]

senses.sense_text:
debauchery, sensuality, lewdness 
intemperance; licentiousness, lasciviousness, sensuality, lewdness Rom. 13:13; insolence, outrageous behavior, Mk. 7:22
